why: Reference point. Graph traversal is pointer-chasing across scattered heap allocations. CPU is memory-bound. Nearly 1 in 5 LLC cache accesses goes to RAM. Priority queue causes 39.9% of all branch mispredictions (Cachegrind). Perf counters measured on --query path only.

Replaced vmulps+vaddps (2 instructions) with _mm512_fmadd_ps (1 instruction) in the AVX-512 hot loop. Fused multiply-add has lower latency and higher throughput. ~4.9% QPS gain at zero Recall cost. Perf counters measured on --query path only.

Arena: replaced ~62500 separate per-node mallocs for upper-layer neighbor lists with one pre-allocated contiguous block. Alignment: padded data slot from 652 to 768 bytes so each vector starts at a 64-byte boundary (AVX-512 cache-line aligned). IPC +6.5% confirms less CPU stalling. LLC-miss rate appears higher but absolute misses are flat — rate rose because total LLC accesses dropped 14% (fewer wasted cache lines per load). Instruction count +14% due to arena memset on loadIndex and larger per-node footprint. Primary benefit is build path (malloc elimination) and distance computation alignment. Query pointer-chasing latency unchanged. Perf counters measured on --query path only.
